task: I’m getting ready for a period when I’ll be handling everything on my own, and I want to make sure the basics don’t slip through the cracks. Can you help me think through a simple way to keep track of regular responsibilities as they come up?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — a simple **“capture and review”** system can work really well when you’re on your own.

### A basic setup
Use **one place** to track everything:
- a notebook
- a notes app
- a simple spreadsheet
- a task app

Then keep just **three lists**:
1. **Today / Next up** — things to do soon
2. **This week** — things coming up
3. **Recurring responsibilities** — regular tasks that repeat

### For recurring basics, track:
- bills / payments
- groceries / household supplies
- laundry / cleaning
- appointments / renewals
- communication you need to keep up with
- maintenance / admin tasks

### Easy routine
- **Daily (2 minutes):** check what needs attention today
- **Weekly (10 minutes):** review the week ahead and refill supplies / plan chores
- **Monthly (15 minutes):** check bills, deadlines, subscriptions, and anything seasonal

### Helpful trick
For each recurring task, write:
- **What it is**
- **How often it happens**
- **What “done” looks like**
- **Any deadline or reminder**

Example:
- Pay electricity bill — monthly — done when paid before due date
- Buy toilet paper — every 2–4 weeks — done when one backup roll remains

### Keep it simple
The goal is not a perfect system — just one you’ll actually use.

If you want, I can help you build a **very simple personal checklist template** you can copy into notes or a planner.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that’s the right general idea. But I’d want it tighter, less cutesy.

I don’t need three pretty lists. I need one working sheet with:
- due date
- what it is
- paid / done or not
- who to call if something goes wrong
- where the paper goes

And I’d want a separate spot for things that are easy to forget — insurance, apartment notices, prescriptions, renewals, that sort of thing.

I already keep notes, but if I’m actually using this, it has to be plain enough to glance at without fuss. Maybe give me a simple template, just the headings, nothing fancy.

turns.agent_response:
Yes — keep it to one working sheet and one “don’t forget” list.

## Working sheet
Use these columns:

- **Due date**
- **What it is**
- **Status** — paid / done / not done
- **Contact if there’s a problem**
- **Where the paper goes**

Optional if useful:
- **Amount**
- **Notes**

## Separate reminder list
Use this for things that are easy to miss:

- insurance
- apartment notices
- prescriptions
- renewals
- licenses / IDs
- subscriptions
- maintenance
- appointments
- mail to review

## Very plain template

### Working sheet
| Due date | What it is | Status | Contact if there’s a problem | Where the paper goes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
|  |  |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |  |

### Easy-to-forget items
| Item | Next due / review date | Notes |
|---|---|---|
|  |  |  |
|  |  |  |

If you want, I can turn this into a **copy-paste notes version** or a **one-page spreadsheet layout**.

user_simulator.text:
Better. Still a little more than I asked for, though.

Just the headings is fine:

Working sheet:
- Due date
- What it is
- Status
- Contact if there’s a problem
- Where the paper goes

Easy-to-forget:
- Item
- Next due / review date
- Notes

That’s the shape I wanted. If you can, make it even plainer next time — no extra explanation hanging off it.

turns.agent_response: Understood. I’ll keep it that plain.
